steps.title: Identify corresponding angles
steps.content: When transversal $l$ intersects two lines, corresponding angles are in identical positions at each intersection. The angle $w°$ (above line $j$, right of $l$) and the angle $54°$ (above line $k$, right of $l$) are corresponding angles.

steps.title: Apply the parallel lines condition
steps.content:
Lines are parallel if and only if corresponding angles are equal. For $j \parallel k$:

$$w = 54$$

Option A directly states $w = 54$, which satisfies this condition.

steps.title: Confirm other options are insufficient
steps.content:
From the angles at $k$:
• The angles $54°$ and $z°$ form a linear pair (supplementary), so $z = 126$ — option D is always true
• The angles $54°$ and $y°$ are vertical angles, so $y = 54$ — option C is always true

Neither tells us the value of $w$, so neither proves parallelism. Option B ($x = 126$) means $w = 126$ (since $w$ and $x$ are vertical angles at $j$), which contradicts the parallelism requirement. That matches choice A — A is correct.
